message:
chore(beads): track Solarized color compat fix and release notes fallback workflow

Add two new bead entries:
- bd-23lq: Fix Solarized Dark / non-truecolor terminal color
  compatibility (GH #101) — color degradation on 16-color terminals
- bd-t8dx: Add release notes fallback workflow for manual releases
  (GH #99) — CI workflow improvement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1zid","title":"TOON: Add BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var support","description":"Add environment variable support for format selection.

## Precedence
1. CLI flag `--format toon` (highest)
2. `BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon`
3. `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon`
4. Default: json

## Implementation
```go
func getRobotFormat() string {
    if format := os.Getenv("BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    if format := os.Getenv("TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT"); format != "" {
        return format
    }
    return "json"
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:19:16.872852012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357129978Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:42.357112285Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var is checked in resolveRobotOutputFormat at main.go:6787","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-220f","title":"Optimize triage unblocks map allocations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:51.675308867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:18:08.460882978Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:18:08.459051197Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-23lq","title":"Fix Solarized Dark / non-truecolor terminal color compatibility (GH #101)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #101 filed by mhpaler (2026-02-07). On macOS Terminal.app with Solarized Dark profile, the right-hand detail pane is rendered with a solid ANSI Green (#2) background making text unreadable. Bottom status bar also has low-contrast color blocks.

## Root Cause Analysis
The codebase uses hex color values everywhere (Dracula theme: #50FA7B for green, #282A36 for dark bg, #1A3D2A for green badge bg). However, Charmbracelet's colorprofile package has a Convert method that downgrades colors: TrueColor → ANSI256 → ANSI (16 colors). When the color profile detects ANSI (16-color):
1. ansi.Convert16(c) maps #50FA7B → ANSI Green (slot #2)
2. In Solarized Dark, ANSI Green (slot #2) is a bright green background color
3. Several background fills use green-adjacent hex colors that all degrade to ANSI Green as background
4. The glamour markdown renderer's docBgPtr is hardcoded to #282a36 which also gets degraded

## Owner Response
Confirmed the issue. Acknowledged that TUI code uses hardcoded ANSI color indices for pane backgrounds.

## Fix Options
1. Use terminal default background (no explicit bg) for pane fills when color profile is < TrueColor
2. Detect color profile at startup and skip background fills in ANSI (16-color) mode
3. Add --no-bg or --theme CLI flag for non-standard palettes
4. Set glamour markdown docBgPtr to nil (no background) when not truecolor

## Key Files
- pkg/ui/theme.go (hex colors via lipgloss.AdaptiveColor)
- pkg/ui/styles.go (background colors: ColorBgDark #1E1F29, ColorBgSubtle #363949, etc.)
- pkg/ui/markdown.go (buildStyleFromTheme(), docBgPtr = #282a36)
- pkg/ui/model.go (lines 5422-5901, status bar extensive background colors)
- vendor/github.com/charmbracelet/colorprofile/profile.go (Convert method, degradation path)

## Considerations
- This is a real bug affecting users on popular terminal configurations (Solarized is very widely used)
- The fix touches styling across multiple files — needs careful testing
- Must not break the experience for truecolor terminal users (most users)
- Consider detecting the color profile once at startup and choosing a style strategy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:38.046656489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:38.046656489Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#101","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","color","terminal-compat","ui"]}

Document includes detailed analysis, implementation roadmap, and success criteria.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b3","depends_on_id":"bd-20y5","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:26:33.631753288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29yy","title":"Profile graph-build allocations (Gonum build path)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:44.927865045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802831432Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:11.802742084Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29yy","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:29:45.228769634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-29yy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Graph-Build Profiling Complete

### Memory Allocation Breakdown (874MB total for 951 iterations)
| Component | Flat | % | Cumulative |
|-----------|------|---|------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 369MB | 42% | 749MB (86%) |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 263MB | 30% | 263MB |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 53MB | 6% | 90MB |
| bufio.NewReaderSize | 50MB | 6% | 50MB |
| set.Ints.Add | 37MB | 4% | 37MB |
| DirectedGraph.NewEdge | 23MB | 3% | 23MB |

### CPU Breakdown
| Component | Cumulative % |
|-----------|-------------|
| NewAnalyzer | 61% |
| DirectedGraph.SetEdge | 27% |
| mapassign_fast64 | 24% |
| mallocgc | 19% |
| GC overhead | 14% |
| DirectedGraph.AddNode | 9% |

### Root Cause
Gonum's DirectedGraph uses **map-backed edge sets** which cause:
1. Heavy allocation in SetEdge (263MB)
2. Map grow/rehash overhead (17% CPU in table.grow)
3. GC pressure from small allocations

### Recommended Optimization (for bd-1wse)
Replace Gonum's DirectedGraph with a compact adjacency-list implementation:
- Pre-allocate node array at known size
- Use []int64 adjacency lists instead of map-backed sets
- Eliminates map overhead entirely

Benchmark: 735KB/op, 4647 allocs/op for 696 issues
","created_at":"2026-01-21T18:01:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ew5","title":"TOON: Add --format flag and Format enum (pkg/robot/format.go)","description":"Add output format infrastructure.

## Files
- `pkg/robot/format.go` (NEW)
- `cmd/bv/main.go` (modify flag definitions)

## Implementation

```go
type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
    FormatAuto Format = "auto"
)

func Encode(payload any, format Format, w io.Writer) error {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        output, err := encodeToon(payload)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        _, err = w.Write([]byte(output))
        return err
    case FormatAuto:
        // Try TOON, fallback to JSON
    default:
        return json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(payload)
    }
}
```

## CLI Flag
```go
outputFormat := flag.String("format", "",
    "Structured output format for --robot-* commands: json or toon")
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:17:45.188873455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:21.449513971Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:21.449495165Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - --format flag exists in main.go:59, Format enum handled via string constant","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-s8wz","title":"TOON: Add E2E shell script tests","description":"E2E verification script for TOON integration.

## File
`scripts/test_bv_toon.sh`

## Test Cases
1. Format flag tests (`--format json`, `--format toon`)
2. Env var tests (`BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT`)
3. Precedence tests (CLI overrides env)
4. Round-trip verification (encode → decode → compare)
5. Token savings measurement (>= 40% target)
6. All 35+ robot commands work with TOON

## Script Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
bv --robot-triage > /tmp/triage.json
bv --robot-triage --format toon > /tmp/triage.toon
tru --decode /tmp/triage.toon > /tmp/decoded.json
diff <(jq -S . /tmp/triage.json) <(jq -S . /tmp/decoded.json)
echo "Round-trip OK"
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:22:15.679571934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:01:12.394667119Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:01:12.394648224Z","close_reason":"Test script completed and passing - test_toon_e2e.sh covers all requirements","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-t8dx","title":"Add release notes fallback workflow for manual releases (GH #99)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #99 filed by qiviut (2026-02-06). Releases created by GoReleaser (via GH Actions) have full changelogs, but manually-created releases have no release notes. Example: v0.14.3 (GoReleaser) has notes, v0.14.4 (manual) has none.

## Owner Response
Confirmed the gap. When GoReleaser fails, binaries are uploaded manually via GitHub UI with minimal description. Proposed adding a fallback workflow triggered on release: [created] events.

## Current State
- Only workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml (triggers on push: tags: ['v*'], runs GoReleaser)
- GoReleaser config: .goreleaser.yaml (changelog: sort: asc, filters: exclude: ['^docs:', '^test:'])
- Beads bd-3v9s and bd-37ki created to track this, but NO actual workflow file was added
- Two commits (e0a1bd8, b50820a) only added beads entries, not implementation

## Implementation Plan
Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml that:
1. Triggers on release: types: [created, edited]
2. Checks if release body is empty or minimal (< 50 characters)
3. Uses GitHub API: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=$TAG
4. Updates release body: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/{id} -X PATCH -f body="$NOTES"
5. Includes idempotency marker (<!-- auto-generated -->) so it doesn't overwrite manually written notes on subsequent edits

## Key Files
- NEW: .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml
- EXISTING: .github/workflows/release.yml (reference for trigger patterns)
- EXISTING: .goreleaser.yaml (reference for changelog config)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","workflow"]}

 {"id":"bd-x1tm","title":"TASK: Normalize robot output envelope + _meta","description":"Problem: robot outputs vary in metadata and envelope fields, making automation brittle.

Goal:
- Standardize a top-level envelope across all robot commands.
- Required fields: generated_at, data_hash, output_format, version; optional _meta with timings/cache/phase2_ready.
- Ensure errors are structured and consistent across commands.

Acceptance:
- All robot outputs share the same envelope keys.
- README/robot-docs updated with envelope spec.
- New tests or golden fixtures validate envelope consistency.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:10:09.421034701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:01:41.283098521Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-friendly","consistency","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x1tm","depends_on_id":"bd-c5g","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:10:09.421034701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xgf7","title":"TOON: Create tru binary wrapper (pkg/robot/toon.go)","description":"Create Go wrapper for toon_rust tru binary.

## File
`pkg/robot/toon.go`

## Implementation
Use shell-out pattern from /data/projects/templates/toon_go_template.go:

```go
func encodeToon(payload any) (string, error) {
    jsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    trPath, err := getToonBinary()
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    cmd := exec.Command(trPath)
    cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(jsonBytes)
    // ...
}
```

## Dependencies
- toon_rust tru binary must be installed
- TOON_TRU_BIN env var for custom path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:16:31.960405372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263276562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263256955Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in cmd/bv/main.go using toon-go package - toonRobotEncoder struct handles encoding","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
